item.title: Army veteran sets off fireworks, starts fire outside NYC federal building, FBI says
item.description: A man was arrested after law enforcement officials said he ignited fireworks, poured flammable liquid and started a fire at 26 Federal Plaza in Lower Manhattan.

item.title: Tate brothers detained as they face new charges, possible U.K. extradition
item.description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate will fight extradition to the U.K., where they face rape and sex trafficking charges.

item.title: Trump to impose 50% tariffs on Canadian hockey sticks, alcohol and other goods
item.description: President Trump is imposing 50% tariffs on a range of Canadian goods, from hockey equipment to alcoholic beverages, escalating the trade dispute between the two countries.

item.title: FDA still probing Taylor Farms lettuce despite false positive test
item.description: The FDA said that as of Sunday, it had not found any "confirmed positive sample results for product testing" in the cyclosporiasis outbreak.

item.title: How the Michigan Senate primary is reflecting Democrats' national divide
item.description: Progressives are getting behind Abdul El-Sayed in Michigan's Democratic Senate primary -- the latest: Sen. Elizabeth Warren of Massachusetts. Political strategists Chuck Rocha and Leslie Shedd join to discuss.

item.title: Vice President Vance and second lady Usha welcome 4th child
item.description: Vice President JD Vance and second lady Usha Vance announced the birth of their fourth child on Sunday. CBS News White House reporter Olivia Rinaldi has the latest.

item.title: New book gives inside account of Trump's impact on Justice Department
item.description: A new book, drawing on sources from inside the Justice Department, details how President Trump has radically altered the DOJ's procedures. New York Times Justice Department reporter Devlin Barrett, author of "The Department of Revenge," joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: Andy Burnham takes the reins as U.K.'s 7th prime minister in a decade
item.description: Having been asked by King Charles III to form a new government, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham has become Britain's 7th prime minister in a decade.

item.title: DOJ is losing cases against immigrants and protesters, New York Times investigation finds
item.description: The federal government has dramatically increased the arrest and charging of people accused of assaulting and impeding officers, but many of these cases have fallen apart, according to a New York Times investigation. Mike McIntire, New York Times investigative reporter, joins to discuss.

item.title: Lindsey Graham's sister, Darline, announces bid for full Senate term in South Carolina
item.description: Darline Graham, the sister of late Sen. Lindsey Graham, says she will run for a full Senate term in South Carolina. CBS News political director Fin Gómez has more.

item.title: NYT moves to quash subpoenas for phone records of reporters and family members
item.description: The New York Times moved to quash subpoenas that sought phone records of the reporters and their relatives, including the spouses of two reporters and one reporter's mother.

item.title: Qatari-gifted Air Force One to be sent off for defensive upgrades, Trump says
item.description: President Trump acknowledged that the new Qatari-gifted Air Force One jet needs security upgrades, saying the process should take about a month to complete. CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang reports.

item.title: Victims of Iranian-sponsored terror call on Trump to add restitution as part of any peace deal
item.description: A delegation of victims of Iranian-sponsored terrorism and their families are in Washington, D.C., demanding that President Trump make Tehran pay restitution as part of any peace deal. CBS News correspondent Jonah Kaplan has the details.

item.title: U.S. launches 10th straight night of strikes on Iran as Pentagon reports nearly 100 injuries in July
item.description: The U.S. on Monday launched its 10th straight night of strikes on Iran, CENTCOM said. The news comes as the Pentagon confirms nearly 100 U.S. service members were hurt in Iranian airstrikes on multiple bases this month. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Aaron MacLean have the latest.

item.title: Funeral held for Nolan Wells as investigation into his death continues
item.description: Family and friends gathered on Monday to pay their final respects to Nolan Wells, the Mississippi teen who was found dead after a Fourth of July boating trip. CBS News correspondent Skyler Henry has the details.

item.title: New details on Manhattan explosion suspect
item.description: The FBI has identified the suspect in Monday morning's explosion outside a federal building in New York City as a 43-year-old U.S. Army veteran. CBS News' Anna Schecter has the latest.

item.title: U.S. launches 10th night of strikes on Iran after 3 U.S. troops killed
item.description: At least 3 U.S. service members were killed over the weekend in Iraq and Jordan, where back-to-back Iranian attacks on a U.S. base seem to have evaded defenses.

item.title: What to know about Taylor Farms amid the cyclospora outbreak
item.description: The nation's largest recent cyclospora outbreak has put one of America's biggest produce suppliers under renewed scrutiny.

item.title: Nearly 100 U.S. service members injured in Iranian strikes this month
item.description: Almost 100 American service members have been injured in strikes on bases in the Middle East this month, U.S. officials said.

item.title: 5 drown in river in Ohio as child runs for help
item.description: Authorities say five people died near Columbus, Ohio, after several of them went into the water to assist another person who was struggling to swim.

item.title: Watch: Officials say suspect in Manhattan explosion had anti-ICE literature
item.description: Law enforcement officials shared new details about the suspect in Monday morning's explosion outside a federal building in New York City. The suspect was described as an "anti-government extremist" who was carrying anti-ICE literature. Following the news conference, CBS News' Anna Schecter provided context.

item.title: Gas prices top $4 a gallon again as U.S.-Iran conflict escalates
item.description: The national average for a gallon of gas reached $4.003 on Monday, the first time in over a month it has crossed that threshold, AAA data shows.

item.title: Judge temporarily halts Paramount-Warner Bros. Discovery merger
item.description: The ruling comes after a coalition of 12 states sued to block the deal, arguing it would harm consumers and the entertainment industry.

item.title: Spain fans rejoice in World Cup win, but some say Trump photo-bombed team
item.description: As Spain's World Cup champs took to the stage with their trophy, President Trump joined them in their moment of glory.

item.title: Darline Graham, Lindsey Graham's sister, says she'll run for full Senate term
item.description: Sen. Darline Graham, who is serving out the remainder of the late Sen. Lindsey Graham's term in the Senate, said she will seek a full term for the South Carolina seat her brother held for more than two decades.

item.title: DOJ denies policy shift after FBI agents told to stand down on ICE assault probes
item.description: HSI is an arm of ICE, meaning the immigration enforcement agency would effectively be investigating incidents involving its own agents.

item.title: Trump to keep using old Air Force One while new plane gets security upgrades
item.description: The new Air Force One "has a lot of capability, but as I understand it, in about a month or so, they're going to send it to have it be maxed out," President Trump said Sunday.

item.title: Pentagon ID's 2 soldiers killed in Iran strike in Jordan; 1 still missing
item.description: U.S. forces launched a new round of airstrikes against Iran at the direction of President Trump following the deaths of two U.S. soldiers in an Iranian strike in Jordan.

item.title: The U.S. labor market may soon face a new crisis: Too few workers
item.description: A severe labor shortage is set to emerge over the next decade amid a wave of baby boomer retirements and a shrinking younger workforce.

item.title: Weather service faces hurricane season with less experienced staff, missing data
item.description: The National Weather Service is hiring hundreds of entry-level employees after losing about 15% of its staff to federal cuts last year.
